components.name: accordion
components.description: Vertically stacked, collapsible content sections

meta.name: Accordion
meta.description: Vertically stacked, collapsible content sections

props.name: type
props.description: Whether one or multiple sections can be open at once ('single' | 'multiple').

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: The initial value when uncontrolled.

props.name: value
props.description: The controlled value.

props.name: onValueChange
props.description: Called with the new value when it changes.

whenToUse: Progressively disclosing sections of related content the user reads top to bottom
Reducing vertical scroll when most sections stay collapsed (FAQs, settings groups)
Allowing multiple sections open at once (type="multiple")
whenNotToUse: Switching between mutually exclusive, equally important views — use Tabs
A single show/hide region — a plain disclosure is enough

antiPatterns.bad: <Accordion> with one item that is always open
antiPatterns.good: Render the content directly, or use a collapsible disclosure
antiPatterns.why: A single permanently-open section adds chrome and indirection for no gain

related.name: Tabs
related.reason: Tabs switch between peer views; Accordion stacks sequential sections vertically

intent.a11yRationale: Each trigger is a native <button> exposing aria-expanded and controlling its panel via aria-controls, so screen readers announce open/closed state and Enter/Space toggle from the platform

flexibility.note: type prop is free to choose based on whether sections are exclusive
Borders, surfaces, and radii must resolve to --cascivo-* semantic tokens

components.name: action-sheet
components.description: Bottom-rising sheet of discrete actions (iOS action-sheet pattern) with a Cancel button

meta.name: ActionSheet
meta.description: Bottom-rising sheet of discrete actions (iOS action-sheet pattern) with a Cancel button

props.name: open
props.description: Whether the component is open (controlled).

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Whether the component is open on first render (uncontrolled).

props.name: onOpenChange
props.description: Called with the next open state when it changes.

props.name: actions
props.description: Choices, each with a label, onSelect, and optional destructive/disabled flags

props.name: title
props.description: Title text for the component.

props.name: description
props.description: Supporting description text.

props.name: showCancel
props.description: When true, shows a cancel button below the actions.

props.name: labels
props.description: Overrides for the component’s user-visible strings (i18n).

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Basic
examples.code:
<ActionSheet
  open={isOpen}
  onOpenChange={setIsOpen}
  title="Share photo"
  actions={[
    { label: 'Copy link', onSelect: copyLink },
    { label: 'Delete', onSelect: remove, destructive: true },
  ]}
/>

examples.title: Without cancel button
examples.code: <ActionSheet defaultOpen showCancel={false} actions={[{ label: "Archive", onSelect: archive }]} />
examples.description: Escape and outside press still dismiss the sheet.

whenToUse: A short list of discrete actions on a touch surface, rising from the bottom
Confirming or choosing among a few operations (e.g. Share, Edit, Delete) on mobile
A mobile-first alternative to an anchored dropdown menu when there is no trigger anchor
whenNotToUse: A single yes/no confirmation — use AlertDialog
A form or scrollable content — use BottomSheet or Sheet
A menu anchored to a trigger on desktop — use Menu or Dropdown

antiPatterns.bad: <ActionSheet actions={[{ label: "OK", onSelect }]} />
antiPatterns.why: A one-action sheet is a confirmation; AlertDialog states the decision clearly

related.name: BottomSheet
related.reason: Use BottomSheet for rich/resizable content; ActionSheet is a fixed list of actions

related.name: Menu
related.reason: Use Menu for a trigger-anchored dropdown on pointer-first surfaces

intent.a11yRationale: Renders role="menu" with role="menuitem" buttons under vertical roving focus (Arrow keys, Home/End, wrapping). The title labels the menu via aria-labelledby (or a built-in label otherwise) and the description via aria-describedby. FocusScope traps and restores focus; DismissableLayer handles Escape and outside-pointer dismissal; a separate Cancel button provides an explicit non-destructive exit.

flexibility.note: Any number of actions; each may be destructive or disabled
Controlled (open/onOpenChange) or uncontrolled (defaultOpen)
showCancel toggles the separate Cancel button (Escape/outside press still dismiss)

components.name: alert
components.description: Highlights a short, important message inline

meta.name: Alert
meta.description: Highlights a short, important message inline

props.name: variant
props.description: Selects the visual style variant.

props.name: title
props.description: Title text for the component.

props.name: icon
props.description: Icon element rendered in the component.

props.name: dismissible
props.description: When true, shows a control to dismiss the component.

props.name: onDismiss
props.description: Called when the component is dismissed.

props.name: action
props.description: Primary action shown in the component.

examples.title: Info
examples.code: <Alert variant="info" title="Heads up">Your trial ends soon.</Alert>

examples.title: Actionable
examples.code: <Alert variant="warning" title="Update available" action={{ label: 'Update now', onClick: update }}>A new version is ready.</Alert>

whenToUse: Surfacing a persistent, contextual message inline within a view (warning, error, info, success)
Communicating status that should stay visible until read or resolved
Offering an inline recovery action tied to the message (action prop)
whenNotToUse: Transient confirmation that should auto-dismiss — use Toast
Blocking the user for a decision — use Modal or AlertDialog

antiPatterns.bad: Auto-dismissing an Alert on a timer to mimic a notification
antiPatterns.why: Alerts persist by design; auto-hiding important inline context risks the user missing it

related.name: Toast
related.reason: Toast auto-dismisses and floats; Alert persists inline in the layout

related.name: AlertDialog
related.reason: Use AlertDialog when the message must block and demand a choice

intent.a11yRationale: role="alert" makes assistive tech announce the message when it appears; the dismiss control is a real button so it is reachable and labeled, and color is paired with an icon/title so meaning is not conveyed by hue alone

content.tone: Direct and specific — state what happened and what to do next
content.notes: Title is a short summary; body explains. Avoid alarmist all-caps.

flexibility.note: Choose the severity variant that matches the message; default is neutral
Severity colors must resolve to --cascivo-color-info/success/warning/destructive

components.name: alert-dialog
components.description: Confirmation dialog requiring explicit user action; no light-dismiss

meta.name: AlertDialog
meta.description: Confirmation dialog requiring explicit user action; no light-dismiss

props.name: open
props.description: Whether the component is open (controlled).

props.name: title
props.description: Title text for the component.

props.name: description
props.description: Supporting description text.

props.name: onConfirm
props.description: Called when the confirm button is activated.

props.name: onCancel
props.description: Called when the cancel button is activated.

props.name: labels
props.description: Overrides for the component’s user-visible strings (i18n).

props.name: variant
props.description: Selects the visual style variant.

examples.title: Destructive confirm
examples.code:
<AlertDialog
  open={isOpen}
  variant="destructive"
  title="Delete project?"
  description="This permanently removes the project and cannot be undone."
  onConfirm={remove}
  onCancel={() => setIsOpen(false)}
/>

examples.title: Custom action labels
examples.code:
<AlertDialog
  open={isOpen}
  title="Sign out?"
  description="Unsaved changes will be lost."
  labels={{ confirm: 'Sign out', cancel: 'Stay' }}
  onConfirm={signOut}
  onCancel={() => setIsOpen(false)}
/>

whenToUse: Confirming a destructive or irreversible action (delete, overwrite, sign out) before it happens
Interrupting a flow to force an explicit decision the user must acknowledge
whenNotToUse: Showing non-critical information or forms — use Modal, which supports light-dismiss and arbitrary content
Confirming a low-stakes action where an undoable Toast is friendlier and less interruptive

antiPatterns.bad: Using AlertDialog for a multi-field form or rich content
antiPatterns.good: Use Modal for content; reserve AlertDialog for a title + description + confirm/cancel decision
antiPatterns.why: AlertDialog only renders a title, description, and two action buttons, and is role="alertdialog" with no light-dismiss — it is built to demand a yes/no answer, not host content

antiPatterns.bad: Adding a backdrop/Escape close to dismiss the dialog without choosing
antiPatterns.why: It uses popover="manual" so it cannot be light-dismissed by design — closing must go through onConfirm/onCancel so the decision is always explicit

related.name: Modal
related.reason: Use for general dialogs with content and light-dismiss; AlertDialog is the must-acknowledge confirm

related.name: Toast
related.reason: Prefer an undoable Toast for reversible low-stakes actions instead of a blocking confirm

related.name: Button
related.reason: A Button typically triggers the action that opens the AlertDialog

intent.a11yRationale: Uses role="alertdialog" with aria-modal and labelled/described-by wiring so assistive tech announces it as an interruptive decision; focus moves to the cancel button on open so the safe default is selected, and popover="manual" prevents accidental dismissal that would skip the decision

content.tone: Direct and specific — name the exact consequence
content.notes: Title states the action ("Delete project?"); description states what is lost and whether it is reversible; confirm label is the verb ("Delete"), not "OK"

flexibility.note: No light-dismiss — closing must go through onConfirm/onCancel
confirm/cancel copy overridable via labels; defaults come from the i18n catalog
Visual styling resolves to semantic --cascivo-color-* tokens; destructive variant uses --cascivo-color-destructive

components.name: app-shell
components.description: Sticky-header + full-height side-nav + single-scroll-container layout with an animated, accessible nav toggle.

meta.name: AppShell
meta.description: Sticky-header + full-height side-nav + single-scroll-container layout with an animated, accessible nav toggle.

props.name: header
props.description: Top bar (typically a ShellHeader). If a React element, its onMenuClick/menuExpanded are bound to the nav toggle.

props.name: nav
props.description: Side navigation (typically a SideNav). Fills full height and scrolls internally.

props.name: children
props.description: Main content, rendered inside the single scroll container.

props.name: footer
props.description: Optional footer pinned below the content area.

props.name: open
props.description: Controlled nav open/visible state. Omit for uncontrolled.

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Initial open state when uncontrolled. Defaults open on desktop, closed on small screens.

props.name: onOpenChange
props.description: Fired when the nav requests open/close (burger, Escape, scrim).

examples.title: Header + SideNav + content
examples.description: The burger in ShellHeader toggles the SideNav automatically.

whenToUse: Building an application screen with a persistent header and side navigation
You want the header burger to toggle the side nav with an animated, accessible show/hide out of the box
You need one scroll container (the main area) with a fixed header and full-height nav
whenNotToUse: Marketing/document pages where the whole page should scroll — use a plain Header instead
A single, always-visible sidebar with no toggle — compose SideNav directly

You need a persisted collapsible sidebar, a top progress bar, or a right aside — that is the richer copy-paste `layout/app-shell` (different props: `sideNav`/`aside`/`persistKey`/`state`), not this drop-in.

antiPatterns.bad: Animating the nav collapse with grid-template-columns: 1fr → 0fr on an auto-width sidebar
antiPatterns.good: AppShell animates an explicit inline-size (desktop) / transform (mobile drawer)
antiPatterns.why: fr units have no definite size to resolve against on a shrink-to-fit flex item, so the track never animates

related.name: layout/app-shell
related.reason: A richer, copy-paste-only app shell with a persisted collapsible sidebar, a signal-driven top progress bar, an error strip, and a right aside. Same `AppShell` name, different prop surface (`sideNav`/`aside`/`persistKey`/`state`). Choose this npm drop-in for a simple header + toggleable nav; choose layout/app-shell when you need persistence/progress/aside.

related.name: ShellHeader
related.reason: Provides the top bar and burger AppShell binds to the nav

related.name: SideNav
related.reason: The side navigation AppShell lays out full-height

intent.a11yRationale: The hidden nav uses transform/inline-size + inert (never display:none alone), so it stays in the a11y tree and is keyboard-reachable when open while leaving the tab order when closed; Escape and a scrim close the mobile drawer and focus returns to the toggle.

components.name: aspect-ratio
components.description: Constrains content to a fixed width-to-height ratio

meta.name: AspectRatio
meta.description: Constrains content to a fixed width-to-height ratio

props.name: ratio
props.description: Width-to-height ratio applied via the CSS aspect-ratio property

props.name: children
props.description: Content to fill the ratio box (typically an image, video, or iframe)

whenToUse: Reserving a stable box for media that loads asynchronously, avoiding layout shift
Embedding responsive iframes (video, maps) at a known proportion
Keeping thumbnails or covers uniform across a grid
whenNotToUse: Content whose height should grow with text — let it size naturally
A single fixed-pixel image where width and height are already known

antiPatterns.bad: Wrapping text content that must expand to fit its lines
antiPatterns.good: Reserve AspectRatio for media; let prose flow at its intrinsic height
antiPatterns.why: A fixed ratio clips or distorts content that needs variable height

related.name: Card
related.reason: Commonly holds a cover image inside a Card media region

intent.a11yRationale: Purely presentational wrapper with no role; the inner media element carries its own semantics and alt text

flexibility.note: Any positive number; common values are 16/9, 4/3, 1, 3/2
The single child is stretched to fill; provide one media element

components.name: avatar
components.description: Displays a user image with initials fallback

meta.name: Avatar
meta.description: Displays a user image with initials fallback

props.name: src
props.description: Image source URL.

props.name: alt
props.description: Alternative text describing the image.

props.name: fallback
props.description: Initials shown when no image is available

props.name: size
props.description: Visual size of the component (e.g. 'sm', 'md', 'lg').

props.name: status
props.description: Status state.

whenToUse: Representing a person or entity with a thumbnail image and initials fallback
Showing presence/status alongside an identity (status prop)
Compactly identifying authors in lists, comments, and headers
whenNotToUse: Decorative imagery unrelated to identity — use a plain
A generic icon or logo with no person/entity meaning

antiPatterns.bad: <Avatar src="/logo.png" /> with no alt and no fallback
antiPatterns.why: Without alt/fallback the identity is invisible to assistive tech and breaks on image load failure

related.name: Status
related.reason: Use Status for a standalone state dot not attached to an identity

intent.a11yRationale: role="img" with alt names the person; on image error it falls back to initials so the identity survives; the status dot carries an accessible label rather than relying on color alone

flexibility.note: Pick the size that fits the surrounding density
Fallback and status colors must resolve to --cascivo-* semantic tokens

components.name: avatar-group
components.description: Overlapping stack of avatars with a max cap and an i18n-labelled +N overflow chip

meta.name: AvatarGroup
meta.description: Overlapping stack of avatars with a max cap and an i18n-labelled +N overflow chip

props.name: max
props.description: Cap the number of visible avatars

props.name: total
props.description: Override the total count used for the +N chip

props.name: spacing
props.description: Spacing between items.

props.name: isGrid
props.description: When true, lays the avatars out in a grid instead of an overlapping stack.

props.name: labels
props.description: Overrides for the component’s user-visible strings (i18n).

whenToUse: Showing several people compactly as an overlapping stack (collaborators, attendees, reviewers)
Capping a long list of avatars with a +N overflow chip
A facepile in a card header, table cell, or activity row
whenNotToUse: A single identity with a name — use User or Avatar
A selectable list of people — use a list/menu with checkboxes

antiPatterns.bad: <AvatarGroup>{hundredsOfAvatars}</AvatarGroup> with no max
antiPatterns.why: Without a max the row grows unbounded; a cap plus total keeps it compact and accurate

related.name: Avatar
related.reason: AvatarGroup arranges Avatar children and adds an overflow chip

related.name: User
related.reason: Use User for a single labelled identity row

intent.a11yRationale: The stack is a labelled group; the +N chip carries an i18n-defaulted aria-label ("{count} more") so the hidden count is announced, not conveyed by the chip text alone

flexibility.note: Pick the overlap that fits the surrounding density
The +N label must come from i18n (builtin.avatarGroup.more or a labels override)

components.name: badge
components.description: Small status label or category indicator

meta.name: Badge
meta.description: Small status label or category indicator

props.name: variant
props.description: Selects the visual style variant.

props.name: size
props.description: Visual size of the component (e.g. 'sm', 'md', 'lg').

whenToUse: Labeling an item with a short, static status or category (e.g. "New", "Beta")
Annotating an element with a count or state that is not interactive
whenNotToUse: A removable or interactive chip (filters, selections) — use Tag
A standalone system state with a colored dot — use Status

antiPatterns.bad: <Badge onClick={removeFilter}>Active</Badge>
antiPatterns.why: Badge is a non-interactive label; interactive/removable labels belong to Tag with proper button semantics

related.name: Tag
related.reason: Tag is the interactive, dismissible counterpart

related.name: Status
related.reason: Status pairs a colored dot with a label for live system state

intent.a11yRationale: role="status" lets assistive tech expose the label as state; meaning is reinforced by text, never by color alone

flexibility.note: Choose the variant that matches the semantic meaning
Colors and radius must resolve to --cascivo-* tokens (--cascivo-radius-badge)

components.name: blockquote
components.description: Quoted passage with optional attribution footer

meta.name: Blockquote
meta.description: Quoted passage with optional attribution footer

props.name: cite
props.description: URL of the quote’s source (sets the HTML cite attribute).

examples.title: With attribution
examples.description: Attribution renders as <footer><cite>

whenToUse: Setting off a quoted passage from another source with visual emphasis
Attributing a quote to a person or work via the cite footer
whenNotToUse: Indenting or emphasizing your own non-quoted text — use Text styling
Inline quotations within a sentence — use a <q> element

antiPatterns.bad: Using <Blockquote> purely to indent a callout
antiPatterns.good: A Card or styled Text block for non-quote emphasis
antiPatterns.why: blockquote semantics tell assistive tech the content is a quotation; misusing it misleads readers

related.name: Prose
related.reason: Prose styles blockquotes among other long-form elements automatically

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a native <blockquote> with the attribution in <footer><cite>, so the quotation and its source are semantically distinct to assistive tech

content.tone: Verbatim quotation; do not paraphrase inside the quote
content.notes: cite names the author or work, not a URL label

flexibility.note: cite is optional; omit when the source is given in surrounding context
Border and text colors must resolve to --cascivo-* tokens

components.name: bottom-sheet
components.description: Mobile bottom sheet with drag-to-resize detents, velocity-projected snapping, and drag-to-dismiss

meta.name: BottomSheet
meta.description: Mobile bottom sheet with drag-to-resize detents, velocity-projected snapping, and drag-to-dismiss

props.name: open
props.description: Whether the component is open (controlled).

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: Whether the component is open on first render (uncontrolled).

props.name: onOpenChange
props.description: Called with the next open state when it changes.

props.name: snapPoints
props.description: Detent heights as ascending fractions of the viewport (0–1)

props.name: activeSnap
props.description: The controlled snap-point index.

props.name: defaultSnap
props.description: The initial snap-point index when uncontrolled.

props.name: onSnapChange
props.description: Called with the new snap-point index when it changes.

props.name: title
props.description: Title text for the component.

props.name: description
props.description: Supporting description text.

props.name: children
props.description: Content rendered inside the component.

props.name: labels
props.description: Overrides for the component’s user-visible strings (i18n).

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Custom detents
examples.description: Snaps between three detents by dragging the handle; opens at half height.

whenToUse: A mobile surface that rises from the bottom and can be resized between detents by dragging
Showing secondary content, filters, or a form where the user can peek at half height then expand
A touch-first overlay that dismisses by flinging or dragging it down past the lowest detent
whenNotToUse: A short yes/no confirmation — use AlertDialog
A non-resizable edge panel without gestures — use Drawer or Sheet
A desktop-first side panel for navigation — use Drawer

antiPatterns.bad: <BottomSheet title="Delete item?">Are you sure?</BottomSheet>
antiPatterns.why: A resizable gesture surface is overkill for a focused yes/no decision

related.name: Sheet
related.reason: Use Sheet for a fixed-height panel from any edge; BottomSheet adds resize detents

related.name: Drawer
related.reason: Use Drawer for a plain edge dialog without resize detents

intent.a11yRationale: Renders role="dialog" with aria-modal; the title labels it via aria-labelledby and the description via aria-describedby. FocusScope traps Tab focus and restores it on close; DismissableLayer handles Escape and outside-pointer dismissal. The grab handle is a labelled separator and a Close button gives a non-gesture dismissal path so the sheet stays keyboard-operable.

flexibility.note: Any ascending list of viewport fractions; the sheet snaps between them
Controlled (open/onOpenChange) or uncontrolled (defaultOpen)
Controlled (activeSnap/onSnapChange) or uncontrolled (defaultSnap)

components.name: breadcrumb
components.description: Shows the current page location within a navigation hierarchy

meta.name: Breadcrumb
meta.description: Shows the current page location within a navigation hierarchy

props.name: items
props.description: The items to render.

props.name: maxVisible
props.description: When items exceed this count, collapse to the first item, an ellipsis, and the trailing items

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description: Accessible label for the component.

examples.title: Basic
examples.code: <Breadcrumb items={[{ label: 'Home', href: '/' }, { label: 'Docs', href: '/docs' }, { label: 'Breadcrumb' }]} />

examples.title: Collapsed
examples.code: <Breadcrumb maxVisible={3} items={[{ label: 'Home', href: '/' }, { label: 'Docs', href: '/docs' }, { label: 'Components', href: '/docs/components' }, { label: 'Breadcrumb' }]} />
examples.description: Long trails collapse to the first item, an ellipsis, and the trailing items.

whenToUse: Showing the user where the current page sits in a navigation hierarchy
Providing one-click navigation back to ancestor pages
Collapsing deep trails to keep the trail compact (maxVisible)
whenNotToUse: Paging through data records — use Pagination
Switching between sibling views — use Tabs

antiPatterns.bad: Using Breadcrumb to step through pages 1, 2, 3 of a dataset
antiPatterns.why: Breadcrumb models hierarchy depth, not sequential position in a list

related.name: Pagination
related.reason: Pagination moves through data pages; Breadcrumb moves up a hierarchy

intent.a11yRationale: Wrapped in <nav> with an aria-label and the current page marked aria-current="page"; ancestor entries are real links so keyboard users can jump up the hierarchy

content.tone: Short labels matching page titles
content.notes: The last item is the current page and is not a link

flexibility.note: Collapse long trails to fit available width
Text colors and focus ring must resolve to --cascivo-* tokens

components.name: button
components.description: Triggers an action or event

meta.name: Button
meta.description: Triggers an action or event

props.name: variant
props.description: Selects the visual style variant.

props.name: size
props.description: Visual size of the component (e.g. 'sm', 'md', 'lg').

props.name: loading
props.description: When true, shows a loading state.

props.name: disabled
props.description: When true, disables the control and removes it from the tab order.

props.name: asChild
props.description: When true, renders the child element as the root via Slot, merging props (polymorphic rendering).

props.name: onClick
props.description: Called when the element is clicked.

examples.title: As link
examples.description: Render button styling on a real anchor (keeps middle-click / open-in-new-tab).

whenToUse: Triggering an action or navigation the user initiates by click/press
Submitting a form or confirming a decision
whenNotToUse: Navigation between pages where a real link is semantically correct — use an anchor
Toggling a binary setting — use Toggle; persistent selection — use Checkbox/Radio

antiPatterns.bad: <Button onClick={() => navigate("/x")}>Home</Button>
antiPatterns.why: Buttons are for actions, links for navigation — assistive tech and the browser treat them differently

related.name: Toggle
related.reason: Use for binary on/off state, not one-shot actions

related.name: Dropdown
related.reason: A button often triggers a menu

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a native <button> so Enter/Space activation, focus, and role come from the platform; loading state uses aria-busy rather than removing the element so focus is preserved

content.tone: Imperative verb-first label
content.notes: Sentence case, ≤3 words; avoid "Click here"

flexibility.note: Visual props must resolve to --cascivo-button-* / semantic tokens
Free, within tone guidance

components.name: button-group
components.description: Visually joins a set of related buttons into a single segmented control

meta.name: ButtonGroup
meta.description: Visually joins a set of related buttons into a single segmented control

props.name: orientation
props.description: Layout orientation of the component.

props.name: size
props.description: Visual size of the component (e.g. 'sm', 'md', 'lg').

props.name: roving
props.description: When true, uses roving tabindex so arrow keys move focus between buttons.

props.name: loop
props.description: When true, navigation wraps around from end to start.

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Accessible label used when no visible label is present.

props.name: aria-labelledby
props.description: Id of the element that labels this component.

whenToUse: Presenting several related, independent actions that read as one control (e.g. an editor toolbar)
Visually grouping buttons that share context without implying mutual exclusivity
whenNotToUse: The buttons represent a single selected value among options — use ToggleGroup
The actions are unrelated and should read as separate buttons — lay them out with spacing instead

antiPatterns.bad: <ButtonGroup><Button>Bold</Button><Button>Italic</Button></ButtonGroup>
antiPatterns.why: A group with no accessible name is announced as an unlabeled region; supply aria-label or aria-labelledby

related.name: ToggleGroup
related.reason: Use when the buttons select a value rather than fire independent actions

related.name: Button
related.reason: A button group is a layout container for buttons

intent.a11yRationale: Exposes role="group" so assistive tech treats the buttons as one labeled set; optional roving focus lets arrow keys traverse the buttons as a single tab stop, matching toolbar expectations

content.tone: Imperative verb-first labels on each child button
content.notes: Keep child labels parallel in form and length so the segmented control reads evenly

flexibility.note: Outer corner radius must resolve to --cascivo-button-radius / --cascivo-radius-control to match standalone buttons
Any focusable controls (Button, IconButton, links) may be grouped

whenToUse: Showing an always-visible month grid for picking a single date (inline, not a popover)
As the building block inside a date-picker or date-range-picker popover
whenNotToUse: A compact form field where an inline grid wastes space — use DatePicker (trigger + popover)
Selecting a contiguous date range — use DateRangePicker, which composes two calendars

antiPatterns.bad: Comparing dates with local-time getters (getHours/getDate) after constructing them with new Date(y, m, d)
antiPatterns.good: All arithmetic and comparisons use UTC getters/Date.UTC so DST and timezone offsets never shift a day
antiPatterns.why: Local-time construction can land a midnight date on the previous day in negative-offset zones, breaking selection and range math

related.name: DatePicker
related.reason: Use the popover-based picker when an inline grid is too heavy for the layout

related.name: DateRangePicker
related.reason: The range picker renders two Calendar instances side-by-side

intent.a11yRationale: Renders an APG-compliant role="grid" table with role="row"/role="gridcell"; a roving tabindex keeps exactly one day focusable (the focused date) so arrow keys move focus per APG, Home/End jump to the week edges, PageUp/PageDown change month (with Shift for year), today exposes aria-current="date", selection sets data-selected with aria-selected on the cell, and out-of-range or predicate-disabled days get aria-disabled and are skipped by selection

flexibility.note: All date props are native Date objects compared in UTC
Weekday labels, month label, and first-day-of-week derive from the locale via Intl (with Monday fallback)
month/year/onViewChange/hideNav let a parent drive navigation (used by DateRangePicker)

Choosing exactly one option from a mutually exclusive set — use Radio

antiPatterns.bad: Two checkboxes for one either/or choice
antiPatterns.good: Use Radio for mutually exclusive options

examples.description: Multi-line shell transcript with terminal-window chrome.

whenToUse: Showing a command, token, or path the user is expected to copy
Displaying a short read-only code block in docs or UI
Inline code references inside flowing text (inline variant)
whenNotToUse: An editable code input — use a textarea or code editor
Whole source files needing folding, diagnostics, or many languages — use a dedicated viewer; the built-in highlighter is a presentational scan for bash/css/js/ts only

antiPatterns.bad: <CodeSnippet variant="inline" code={"line1\nline2"} />
antiPatterns.why: The inline variant is a single in-text chip; multi-line content belongs in the multi variant

related.name: CopyButton
related.reason: CodeSnippet embeds the same clipboard behavior for its copy affordance

intent.a11yRationale: Code is rendered in semantic <code>/<pre>. The copy button is a real button with an aria-label that flips between the copy and copied messages so the action and its result are announced. Line numbers are aria-hidden so they are not read as content.

flexibility.note: inline | single | multi — controls the wrapping element and layout
Shown by default for single/multi; togglable via showCopyButton

examples.code:
<Combobox
  label="Country"
  options={[
    { value: 'us', label: 'United States' },

whenToUse: Single-select from a long list where type-to-filter makes finding an option faster
Form fields where the value is one of many known options (country, assignee, repository)
whenNotToUse: Short option lists (≈2–7) where filtering adds no value — use Select or SegmentedControl
Selecting multiple values — use MultiSelect
Triggering actions or commands — use Dropdown or CommandMenu

antiPatterns.bad: Using Combobox to fire actions like "Delete" or "Export"
antiPatterns.good: Use Dropdown for actions; Combobox is for picking a value
antiPatterns.why: Combobox has role="combobox" with a listbox of selectable values and onChange semantics — actions belong in a menu

antiPatterns.bad: Setting searchable={false} on a 200-item list
antiPatterns.why: Without filtering the list becomes an unusable scroll; either keep search on or switch to a different selection pattern

related.name: Select
related.reason: Use for short lists that do not need type-to-filter

related.name: MultiSelect
related.reason: Use when more than one value can be selected

related.name: CommandMenu
related.reason: Use the Cmd+K palette for command/navigation search rather than value selection

intent.a11yRationale: Trigger exposes role="combobox" with aria-expanded, aria-controls, aria-haspopup="listbox", and aria-activedescendant pointing at the active option so screen readers track focus through arrow navigation without moving DOM focus off the input; error state is wired via aria-invalid + aria-describedby and announced with role="alert"

flexibility.note: Filtering can be toggled off for short lists via searchable={false}
Supports value + onChange or defaultValue
Listbox/field styling resolves to semantic --cascivo-color-* / --cascivo-radius-* tokens

examples.code:
<CommandMenu
  open={open}
  onOpenChange={setOpen}
  groups={[
    {
      heading: 'Actions',
      items: [
        { id: 'new', label: 'New file', shortcut: ['⌘', 'N'], onSelect: createFile },
        { id: 'search', label: 'Search docs', keywords: ['find'], onSelect: openSearch },
      ],
    },
  ]}
/>

whenToUse: Giving power users a global Cmd/Ctrl+K palette to search and run actions or navigate
Exposing many commands across grouped categories that benefit from fuzzy search
Multi-step flows where selecting an item drills into a nested page of further commands
whenNotToUse: Picking a form value from a list — use Combobox
A small set of actions tied to a specific trigger button — use Dropdown

antiPatterns.bad: Using CommandMenu as a form field to capture a selected value
antiPatterns.good: Use Combobox for value selection inside a form
antiPatterns.why: CommandMenu is a modal command palette whose items run onSelect callbacks or open sub-pages; it has no controlled form value

antiPatterns.bad: Items with both onSelect and page set, or neither
antiPatterns.why: Each item must do exactly one thing — run an action (onSelect) or push a nested page (page)

related.name: Combobox
related.reason: Use for filtering and selecting a form value rather than running commands

related.name: Dropdown
related.reason: Use for a small action menu anchored to a button instead of a global palette

related.name: Modal
related.reason: Renders inside a native modal dialog with backdrop dismiss

intent.a11yRationale: The search input is role="combobox" with aria-controls, aria-expanded, aria-autocomplete="list" and aria-activedescendant so navigation is announced without moving DOM focus from the input; it renders in a native <dialog> for focus trapping, the empty/loading state uses role="status", and footer hints are aria-hidden as redundant decoration

content.tone: Action-oriented item labels; short group headings
content.notes: Item labels are verb-first commands; use keywords for synonyms users might type

flexibility.note: Global Cmd/Ctrl+K binding can be disabled via hotkey={false} for custom triggering
onQueryChange + loading allow fetching items per keystroke
Palette styling resolves to semantic --cascivo-color-* / --cascivo-radius-modal tokens

whenToUse: Grouping a short set of related rows under a visible heading in a bordered surface
Presenting selectable or navigable rows (settings groups, member lists)
Separating a labelled cluster of items from surrounding page content
whenNotToUse: A long, virtualised data set — use a Table or DataList instead
Plain bulleted prose where the container border adds no meaning — use List

antiPatterns.good: Use ContainedListItem asChild with a <button> or <a> so the row is keyboard-focusable
antiPatterns.why: A non-interactive div gives no focus or role semantics to assistive tech

related.reason: List is for unbordered prose lists; ContainedList adds a labelled surface

related.name: ContainedListItem
related.reason: Each row is a ContainedListItem

intent.a11yRationale: The container heading labels the ul/li structure; interactive rows must slot a real button or link via asChild so role and focus are native, not faked on a div

components.name: context-menu
components.description: Right-click context menu anchored at pointer coordinates via CSS custom properties

meta.name: ContextMenu
meta.description: Right-click context menu anchored at pointer coordinates via CSS custom properties

props.name: children
props.description: The right-click target first, followed by ContextMenuItem children.

whenToUse: Offering actions contextual to an element via right-click (rename, delete, copy on a row or canvas item)
Power-user surfaces where the desktop right-click affordance is expected
whenNotToUse: Primary actions that must be discoverable by all users — right-click is hidden; use a visible Button or Dropdown
Selecting a value or filtering a list — use Combobox/Select

antiPatterns.bad: Putting the only path to an action behind a right-click ContextMenu
antiPatterns.good: Also expose it via a visible Dropdown or Button; treat the context menu as a shortcut
antiPatterns.why: Right-click is undiscoverable and unavailable on touch, so critical actions become unreachable for many users

flexibility.note: Accepts arbitrary ContextMenuItem children after the trigger child

whenToUse: Letting users copy a short value to the clipboard — install commands, API keys, tokens, share links

whenNotToUse: Triggering a general action — use Button
Copying content the user can already select and edit freely where a copy affordance adds nothing

related.name: Button
related.reason: Use for actions other than copying to clipboard

related.reason: Often placed alongside a read-only field whose value it copies

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a native <button> so Enter/Space, focus, and role come from the platform; the aria-label swaps between copy and copied labels to announce the state change to screen readers since the icon swap alone is silent, and the SVG icons are aria-hidden

When two months visible at once and hover-preview of the in-between span aid selection
whenNotToUse: Picking a single date — use DatePicker or Calendar

flexibility.note: value/defaultValue are { start: Date; end: Date } with native Date objects compared in UTC

Display, weekday labels, and week start derive from the locale via Intl

related.name: FileUploader

whenNotToUse: A single primary action per row — render it inline as a Button

flexibility.note: Any number of leading and/or trailing actions; each may be destructive

examples.code:
<Timeline
  items={[
    { id: '1', title: 'Order placed', time: '09:00', status: 'complete' },
    { id: '2', title: 'Shipped', time: '12:30', status: 'current' },
    { id: '3', title: 'Delivered', status: 'upcoming' },
  ]}
/>

whenToUse: Showing an ordered sequence of events over time (activity feeds, order tracking)
Communicating progress through discrete stages with complete/current/upcoming status
Presenting a chronological history where order is meaningful
whenNotToUse: Interactive multi-step navigation the user controls — use Tabs or a Stepper
Unordered lists where sequence carries no meaning — use List

antiPatterns.bad: Marking several items as status="current" at once
antiPatterns.good: Exactly one current item so aria-current="step" points to a single position
antiPatterns.why: Multiple current markers make the active position ambiguous for assistive tech

related.name: List
related.reason: List is for unordered prose; Timeline conveys ordered, time-stamped events

intent.a11yRationale: Rendered as an ordered list (ol/li) to convey sequence; the active item carries aria-current="step" and decorative markers are aria-hidden so screen readers read only the text

flexibility.note: vertical for feeds, horizontal for compact progress strips
Use a single current item; status drives marker colour via data-status

components.name: toast
components.description: Transient notification surfaced via the useToast hook

meta.name: Toast
meta.description: Transient notification surfaced via the useToast hook

props.name: title
props.description: Title text for the component.

props.name: description
props.description: Supporting description text.

props.name: variant
props.description: Selects the visual style variant.

props.name: duration
props.description: How long (ms) the toast stays visible before auto-dismiss.

examples.title: Trigger
examples.code:
const { toast } = useToast()
toast({ title: "Saved", variant: "success" })

whenToUse: Confirming that a background or just-completed action succeeded or failed (e.g. "Saved")
Brief, transient feedback that should auto-dismiss without interrupting the task
Non-blocking notifications that can stack and disappear on their own
whenNotToUse: A persistent, inline message tied to a specific region of the page — use Alert
Feedback that requires the user to acknowledge or decide before continuing — use Modal or AlertDialog

antiPatterns.bad: toast({ title: "Delete this account?", duration: 0 })
antiPatterns.why: Toasts auto-dismiss and are easy to miss; a destructive decision needs a blocking dialog the user must confirm

related.name: Alert
related.reason: Use for persistent inline status that stays until resolved

related.name: Modal
related.reason: Use when the user must acknowledge before proceeding

intent.a11yRationale: Toasts render into a labelled region; non-critical ones use role="status" with aria-live="polite", while destructive variants escalate to role="alert" with aria-live="assertive" so urgency is conveyed without stealing focus.

content.tone: Short, past-tense outcome for the title; optional one-line detail in the description
content.notes: Title should read on its own (e.g. "Saved"); avoid stuffing actions into a transient toast

flexibility.note: Limited to default | success | warning | destructive — drives color and live-region urgency
Consumer can tune auto-dismiss timing (default 5000ms)

components.description: On/off switch built as an accessible button

props.description: Whether the control is checked (controlled).

props.description: Whether the control is checked on first render (uncontrolled).

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called when the value changes.

props.description: Visible text label beside the switch; it also becomes the accessible name. When a heading already labels the control, omit this and pass aria-label instead to avoid duplicated text.

props.name: size
props.description: Visual size of the component (e.g. 'sm', 'md', 'lg').

props.name: disabled
props.description: When true, disables the control and removes it from the tab order.

whenToUse: Flipping a single binary setting that takes effect immediately (e.g. notifications on/off)
On/off state where a physical switch metaphor reads better than a checkmark
Settings screens where the change applies live without a separate submit
whenNotToUse: Selecting items in a form that is submitted later — use Checkbox
Choosing one option among several mutually exclusive labels — use SegmentedControl or Radio

antiPatterns.bad: <Toggle label="I accept the terms" /> inside a submitted form
antiPatterns.why: A switch implies an instant on/off setting; agreement that is submitted with the form is a checkbox

antiPatterns.bad:
Dark mode

— the label repeats the heading
antiPatterns.why: The `label` prop renders visible text and is the accessible name; when a heading already names the control, use aria-label so the name is not shown (and read) twice

related.name: Checkbox
related.reason: Use for form selections submitted later rather than instant settings

related.name: SegmentedControl
related.reason: Use when choosing among several exclusive options, not just on/off

intent.a11yRationale: Renders a <button role="switch"> with aria-checked reflecting state, so assistive tech announces it as a switch and Space/Enter toggle it via native button activation.

flexibility.note: Track and thumb colors must resolve to --cascivo-color-* / radius / focus-ring tokens
Optional label describing the setting being toggled

whenToUse: Choosing one value from a small, mutually exclusive set that should stay visible (type="single")
Toggling several independent options in a compact segmented control (type="multiple")
whenNotToUse: The options are independent actions, not a selection — use ButtonGroup
There are many options or they need labels and descriptions — use Radio or Checkbox groups

antiPatterns.bad: <ToggleGroup type="single" items={items} onValueChange={save} />
antiPatterns.why: Single-select used as a controlled value should be driven by value/onValueChange so the source of truth lives with the parent

related.name: ButtonGroup
related.reason: Use when the buttons fire independent actions rather than select a value

related.name: Toggle
related.reason: Use a single Toggle for one standalone binary option

intent.a11yRationale: Single mode renders role="radiogroup" with role="radio" + aria-checked items; multiple mode uses a plain group of buttons with aria-pressed. Roving tabindex makes the set one tab stop and arrow keys move between items, matching the APG radiogroup/toolbar patterns

content.tone: Short noun or icon per option
content.notes: Keep item labels parallel and concise; provide a label or icon meaningful out of context

flexibility.note: Item height must resolve to --cascivo-control-height-* to align with other controls
Items may use a text label, an icon, or both; consumer owns the icon set

props.description: The popover content — interactive and selectable

props.description: Side and alignment of the bubble relative to the trigger

props.description: Initial open state when uncontrolled

props.description: Called whenever the open state should change

  Your password must contain at least 12 characters.
</Toggletip>

examples.title: Controlled
examples.description: Drive the open state from the parent
examples.code: } open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen} placement="bottom-start" > Learn more

whenToUse: Revealing brief supplementary information on demand when the user clicks an info affordance
Content that must be selectable or contain a link, where a hover Tooltip would be unreachable
Touch-friendly hint surfaces where hover is unavailable
whenNotToUse: A passive label that should appear on hover/focus without a click — use Tooltip
A blocking, focus-trapping dialog for a task — use Modal
Rich forms or pickers anchored to a trigger — use Popover
A list of one-shot actions behind a button — use MenuButton

antiPatterns.bad: Using a hover Tooltip to hold a link or text the user needs to read or copy
antiPatterns.good: Use a Toggletip so the content stays open on click and remains reachable and selectable
antiPatterns.why: Hover tooltips disappear on pointer-out and are not keyboard- or touch-reachable for interaction, so interactive or copyable content needs a click-toggled bubble

antiPatterns.bad: Giving the trigger an icon with no text and no labels.label override
antiPatterns.good: Provide labels.label or rely on the built-in i18n accessible name
antiPatterns.why: An icon-only button with no accessible name cannot be operated by screen-reader or voice users

related.name: Tooltip
related.reason: Use Tooltip for non-interactive hints shown on hover/focus rather than click

related.name: Popover
related.reason: Use Popover for richer interactive content like forms or pickers

related.name: MenuButton
related.reason: Use MenuButton when the trigger should reveal a list of actions

intent.a11yRationale: The trigger is a real <button> with aria-expanded reflecting open state and aria-controls pointing at the bubble; an icon-only trigger gets an accessible name from labels.label or the built-in i18n catalog. The bubble is role="status" so its content is announced when it opens, and unlike a hover tooltip the content remains visible and reachable for selection and links. DismissableLayer provides Escape and outside-pointer dismissal, and high-contrast and reduced-motion preferences are honored in CSS.

flexibility.note: Trigger and bubble styling must resolve to the listed --cascivo-* tokens
trigger and children accept arbitrary ReactNode supplied by the consumer
Use uncontrolled (defaultOpen) or controlled (open + onOpenChange) as needed

content.notes: No essential-only information; keep it to a few words and avoid full sentences

whenToUse: Showing nested, parent/child data the user expands and collapses (file trees, org charts, nav)
Letting the user pick one or several nodes from a hierarchy
Navigating deep structures where indentation communicates depth
whenNotToUse: A flat, non-nested list of choices — use List or StructuredList
A small set of mutually exclusive sections of prose — use Accordion

antiPatterns.bad: A single-level TreeView with no children on any node
antiPatterns.why: A tree with no hierarchy adds twisties and ARIA tree semantics for content that is flat

related.name: Accordion
related.reason: Accordion stacks a few sequential disclosure sections; TreeView models arbitrary-depth hierarchy

related.name: StructuredList
related.reason: StructuredList is flat tabular rows; TreeView is nested with expand/collapse

intent.a11yRationale: Implements the WAI-ARIA TreeView pattern: role=tree on the root, role=treeitem with aria-level/posinset/setsize and aria-expanded on each node, a single roving tabindex, and full APG keyboard support (arrows expand/collapse and move, Home/End, Enter/Space select, printable-character typeahead)

flexibility.note: single vs multi is the consumer’s choice based on the interaction
Arrow/Home/End/typeahead behavior follows the APG tree pattern and must not be re-mapped
Per-level indent is driven by --cascivo-tree-indent and may be overridden

whenToUse: Editing code or config inline — JSON, snippets, web languages — with line numbers and syntax colors
A lightweight, themeable code field where a full IDE editor (Monaco/CodeMirror) would be overkill
Editing Markdown notes — find/replace, real undo/redo, save, and selection-preserving external sync
Editing long-form Markdown — generated docs, concatenated books, big notes — windowed (viewport-scoped) tokenization keeps scrolling/typing smooth well past ~5,000 lines
whenNotToUse: You need IntelliSense/LSP, multi-cursor, folding, a minimap, or diff view — use a full editor framework
Plain prose or a single-line value — use Textarea or Input

Sustained editing of 100k+-line documents — use a full editor framework / dedicated worker pipeline (the windowed tokenizer keeps per-render work O(viewport), but a worker offload is intentionally out of scope)
Soft-wrap (wrap) on a very large document — rendering is O(n) under wrap; disable wrap above ~10k lines for sustained editing

antiPatterns.bad: Reaching for Monaco to show an editable snippet
antiPatterns.good: Use CodeEditor for the basic set (line numbers + highlighting) at a fraction of the weight
antiPatterns.why: The overlay technique keeps the bundle tiny and themes via the cascivo token system

related.name: Highlight
related.reason: The read-only renderer sharing the same tokenizer — for snippets and docs

related.name: Textarea
related.reason: Use for free-form prose without syntax highlighting

intent.a11yRationale: The native <textarea> is the editing surface, so caret, selection, IME, undo, and the a11y tree come from the browser; the highlight layer and gutter are aria-hidden.

flexibility.note: Ships a small grammar set; registerGrammar adds custom languages without bundle bloat

Windowed tokenization (tokenizeRange + LineStateIndex) makes per-render work O(viewport) and per-edit work O(changed suffix); long Markdown edits well past ~5,000 lines. Worker offload / 100k+-line sustained editing stay out of scope
